constraints.coverage_assumption: The user is asking about Kingma & Ba 2014 "Adam" and Vaswani et al. 2017 "Attention Is All You Need", both present in the corpus. I assume both papers are in the fixed inventory and will ground the answer in them; any motivation for why the Transformer authors chose 0.98 will only be reported if the paper itself states it.

task.object: contradiction between Adam beta2=0.999 (common default) and Transformer paper beta2=0.98

entities: adam default beta2 value and supporting context
transformer paper beta2 value and supporting context
explicit explanation in the Transformer paper if any for its beta2 choice
how the two values can both be true (context distinction)
normalized_question: Resolve the conflict between the commonly cited Adam beta2=0.999 default and the claim that the Transformer paper used beta2=0.98, by retrieving the beta2 values actually reported in the Adam paper and in the Transformer paper, plus any explicit acknowledgement of deviation, and explaining how both can be true.

obligations.description: What beta2 value is reported as the default in the Adam paper?
What beta2 value is reported for the Adam optimizer in the Transformer (Attention Is All You Need) paper?
Distinguish the contexts: Adam paper's recommended default vs. Transformer paper's task-specific chosen value; identify whether the conflict is a contradiction or a scope/methodology difference.

raw_question: Some sources say Adam beta2 is 0.999, but the Transformer paper used 0.98. Resolve this conflict.
